answer:
In Allama Iqbal's poem *Zuhd aur Rindi* (*Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 44), the ascetic (the cleric/Maulvi, embodying *zuhd*) and the wine-drinker/libertine (*rindi*, embodied by the poet Iqbal) are juxtaposed throughout the narrative. The cleric—described with Sufi airs yet tainted by prideful "dregs" in his "pious wine"[^1]—gossips critically about Iqbal as a paradoxical figure who blends saintly observance with sinful indulgence. This reaches its height in the cleric's quoted speech, which puzzles over Iqbal's hybrid life of prayer and revelry,[^2] and in the response portraying Iqbal as simultaneously knowing "the Sinner’s path" and "the Law’s decree."[^3]

Key juxtaposed lines include:

> For many years he was a neighbor mine,  
> So Saint and Sinner would their paths align.[^4]

> “He sings at night, at dawn recites God’s Word;  
> The meaning of this riddle is unheard.”[^5]

> “A paradox in flesh, not Iqbal’s whole,  
> A sage’s mind, a melancholy soul.  
> “He knows the Sinner’s path, the Law’s decree,  
> In mystic lore, a new Mansur is he.[^6]

The poem closes on self-reflective ambiguity, with Iqbal lamenting his own unknowability.[^7]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, [*Zuhd aur Rindi*](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/44-zuhd-aur-rindi/translation/#p-f39e58).

[^2]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, [*Zuhd aur Rindi*](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/44-zuhd-aur-rindi/translation/#p-5cbc5a).

[^3]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, [*Zuhd aur Rindi*](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/44-zuhd-aur-rindi/translation/#p-ea39dc).

[^4]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, [*Zuhd aur Rindi*](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/44-zuhd-aur-rindi/translation/#p-34574a).

[^5]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, [*Zuhd aur Rindi*](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/44-zuhd-aur-rindi/translation/#p-5cbc5a).

[^6]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, [*Zuhd aur Rindi*](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/44-zuhd-aur-rindi/translation/#p-f9aaa0).

[^7]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, [ch. 44 *Zuhd aur Rindi*](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/44-zuhd-aur-rindi/translation/#p-b1ce70).
